summary: Typosquat republish of bytecodealliance/wasmtime-py under forged identity with opaque native binary
details: Package is published as `pgrayy-wasmtime` but its METADATA claims authorship by `The Wasmtime Project Developers <hello@bytecodealliance.org>` and points homepage at bytecodealliance/wasmtime-py. The shipped source tree is a verbatim copy of the legitimate `wasmtime` PyPI package: `top_level.txt` is `wasmtime`, so `pip install pgrayy-wasmtime` registers the `wasmtime` import namespace and shadows the real package. The installed package loads a single sole-platform 31 MB native dylib at `wasmtime/darwin-aarch64/_libwasmtime.dylib` via `cdll.LoadLibrary` from `wasmtime/_ffi.py` line 38 on import. The binary's build-path string references a GitHub Actions runner path (`/Users/runner/work/wasm-deps/...`) but is not verifiable against any upstream bytecodealliance release hash, and no other architecture is shipped. The combination of forged publisher identity, namespace collision with a legitimate widely-used package, and an unauditable native binary loaded on import constitutes a typosquat-republish supply-chain pattern: any installer who pip-installs this package and imports `wasmtime` is executing arbitrary native code under a stolen identity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
